purpose: Provisional selection of utility weights (alpha, beta, gamma) for the Stage 2 proof-of-concept baseline. These are PLACEHOLDERS; Stage 3 calibrates alpha/beta/gamma from the IO Archive. Selection criterion (from the handoff): a non-degenerate, sub-saturating cascade with run-to-run variation, leaving headroom for Stage 6 interventions.
finding: Unit weights (1,1,1) are supercritical: ~60% infected, 90% reached in 4 steps, because the alpha*c_v*attention term gives high-credulity nodes a large exposure-independent utility so they share on first contact. beta (peer-influence weight) is the dominant lever on final size.
rationale: ~14% spread (~65K nodes) matches the order of magnitude of real misinformation reach (Vosoughi et al. report false news reaching up to ~100K), peaks around step 6, terminates well before T_max=200, shows run-to-run variation (std ~4,271), and leaves substantial headroom for interventions. Final values are subject to replacement by Stage 3 calibration.
